stem: In triangle PQR, cos(Q) = 9/41 and angle P is a right angle. What is the value of cos(R)?
rationale: Since angle P is a right angle, angles Q and R are complementary, so cos(R) = sin(Q). From cos(Q) = 9/41, using sin²Q + cos²Q = 1, sin(Q) = √(1 − 81/1681) = √(1600/1681) = 40/41. Therefore cos(R) = 40/41 ≈ 0.9756.
